description: Phase 2 — Interactive Windgram Visualizations. Build dynamic, interactive windgram-style charts that show atmospheric profiles over time at any location. These replace static windgram images with responsive, interactive web components. Washington State launch sites are the primary testing ground.

context.stack: Next.js 16 + Turso (Drizzle ORM) + shadcn/ui + Leaflet + Tailwind v4
context.weatherAPI: Open-Meteo pressure-level data already fetched by src/lib/weather-profile.ts
context.existingData: AtmosphericProfile type with hourly data at 8 pressure levels (1000-500 hPa), surface data, computed lapse rates, thermal index, cloud base estimates
context.windgramReference: Traditional RASP windgrams show: X-axis=time (hours), Y-axis=altitude/pressure, wind barbs at each level/hour, background color=lapse rate (stability), thermal velocity at top, cloud symbols, humidity hatching. See http://wxtofly.net/v2/windgrams.html for reference.
context.designValues: Beautiful yet minimalistic. Friendly UIs. Strong UX focus. Non-technical pilots should understand it at a glance. Power users can dig into raw data.

userStories.title: Windgram Chart Component — Core Layout
userStories.description: Build the foundational windgram chart component using a canvas-based or SVG approach. This is the core visual: a time × altitude grid with proper axes, labels, and responsive sizing.
acceptanceCriteria: New component at src/components/windgram/windgram-chart.tsx
X-axis: time (hours of day, e.g. 6AM-8PM local time)
Y-axis: altitude in meters AND feet (dual labels), with pressure levels marked
Grid lines at each hour and each pressure level
Responsive: works on mobile (360px) through desktop (1400px)
Accepts AtmosphericProfile data as props
Uses either D3.js, visx, or HTML Canvas — choose what's best for performance and interactivity
Light and dark theme support (use CSS variables from the existing theme)
Loading skeleton state while data fetches
npm run build succeeds
npm run lint passes
userStories.notes: Performance matters — this chart will re-render on interactions. Canvas may be better than SVG for the background fill. Consider using visx (React bindings for D3) for a more React-native approach. The chart should feel smooth on mobile.

userStories.title: Windgram — Lapse Rate Background
userStories.description: Add the most important visual layer: background color fill showing atmospheric stability (lapse rate) at each altitude/time cell. This is what tells a pilot at a glance whether thermals will form.
acceptanceCriteria: Each cell (time × altitude) colored by lapse rate

Color scale: unstable (warm orange/yellow, lapse rate >= 3°C/1000ft) → neutral (white/light gray, ~2°C/1000ft) → stable (cool blue/purple, <= 1°C/1000ft) → inversion (deep purple, negative lapse rate)
Color scale is visually intuitive — warm colors = good for flying, cool colors = bad
Smooth gradient between cells (not blocky)
Legend showing the color scale with labels: 'Unstable', 'Neutral', 'Stable', 'Inversion'
Lapse rate data comes from the computed derivedParams in AtmosphericProfile
npm run build succeeds
Unit test for color mapping function
userStories.notes: The lapse rate between levels is already computed in weather-profile.ts. Use bilinear interpolation for smooth gradients between the discrete pressure levels and hours. The dry adiabatic lapse rate is ~3°C/1000ft — anything above that is unstable (good thermals).

userStories.title: Windgram — Wind Barbs
userStories.description: Add wind barb symbols at each pressure level and time step, showing wind speed and direction at a glance — the signature element of a windgram.
acceptanceCriteria: Wind barbs rendered at each pressure level for each hour
Standard meteorological wind barb convention: shaft points into wind direction, half barb = 5 kt, full barb = 10 kt, flag = 50 kt
Wind speed in knots (convert from km/h in the data)
Barbs sized appropriately — not too dense, readable on mobile
On mobile: optionally thin out barbs (show every other level or every other hour) to avoid clutter
Color-coded by speed: light winds (green), moderate (yellow), strong (orange), dangerous (red)
Tooltip on hover/tap showing exact wind speed, direction, and altitude
npm run build succeeds
Unit test for wind barb rendering logic (knots to barb configuration)
userStories.notes: Wind barbs are SVG elements overlaid on the chart. The km/h to knots conversion is: knots = km/h * 0.539957. Standard barb rules: speed rounded to nearest 5 kt, then decompose into flags (50), full barbs (10), half barbs (5).

userStories.title: Windgram — Thermal & Cloud Indicators
userStories.description: Add visual indicators for thermal activity, cloud base, top of lift, and boundary layer height — the key soaring-specific information.
acceptanceCriteria: Thermal velocity shown as text/icons along the top of the chart for each hour (like windgrams show 'W*')
Color-coded thermal strength: none (gray), weak <1m/s (light blue), moderate 1-2m/s (green), strong >2m/s (orange)
Cloud base line drawn across the chart showing estimated cloud base altitude over time
Top of lift line showing maximum useful thermal height
Boundary layer height line (from surface data)
Humidity/cloud layer shading: areas where relative humidity > 90% at a pressure level get a subtle cloud overlay pattern
These indicators are visually distinct but don't overwhelm the lapse rate background
npm run build succeeds
userStories.notes: Cloud base, top of lift, and thermal velocity are already computed in weather-profile.ts derivedParams. The boundary layer height comes from surface data. Keep these overlays subtle — dashed lines, semi-transparent fills. The lapse rate background should remain the primary visual.

userStories.title: Windgram — Interactivity & Detail Panel
userStories.description: Make the windgram interactive: hover/tap to see detailed data, zoom, and switch between days.
acceptanceCriteria: Hover crosshair that follows mouse/finger showing current time and altitude
Detail panel (side or bottom) showing exact values at the crosshair position: temperature, wind speed/direction, humidity, lapse rate, thermal index
Click/tap to pin the crosshair and keep the detail panel open
Day selector tabs to switch between forecast days (up to 7 days)
Smooth transitions when switching days
Touch-friendly: works with finger on mobile, not just mouse
Keyboard accessible: arrow keys move crosshair
npm run build succeeds
npm run lint passes
userStories.notes: The detail panel is crucial for non-technical pilots — they hover over the chart and get plain-language explanations. Consider using a floating tooltip on desktop and a fixed bottom sheet on mobile.

userStories.title: Windgram — Simple Flyability Summary
userStories.description: Above the windgram chart, show a simple at-a-glance flyability assessment for pilots who don't want to interpret the chart themselves.
acceptanceCriteria: Summary bar above the windgram for each day
Shows: flyability rating (Epic/Great/Good/Fair/Poor with color), best window (e.g. '11AM-3PM'), key concerns (e.g. 'Strong upper winds', 'Low cloud base')
Plain language: 'Looks like a great XC day! Best window 11AM-3PM. Watch for increasing winds after 2PM.'
Uses existing scoring algorithm plus atmospheric profile data for richer assessment
Expandable to show more detail (wind at launch altitude, thermal strength estimate)
Designed for the pilot who just wants to know: should I drive to launch today?
npm run build succeeds
userStories.notes: This is the UX layer that makes SoarCast accessible to non-weather-nerds. The windgram is the power tool; this summary is the quick answer. Use the atmospheric profile data to generate more nuanced assessments than the current simple scoring.

userStories.title: Integrate Windgrams into Site Pages
userStories.description: Wire the windgram component into the site detail pages so users can see atmospheric profiles for any launch site or custom location.
acceptanceCriteria: Site detail page (/sites/[slug]) shows windgram as the hero visualization
Flyability summary above the windgram
Custom site pages (/sites/custom/[id]) also show windgram
Map click: clicking any point on the browse map (/sites/browse) shows a windgram for that location (bottom sheet or modal)
Loading states while atmospheric data fetches
Error states if Open-Meteo is unavailable
The old simple forecast table/cards are available in an 'Advanced' or 'Table View' tab
npm run build succeeds
npm run lint passes
Playwright E2E test: navigate to a WA launch site, verify windgram renders
userStories.notes: The windgram fetch can be slow (multiple pressure levels). Use React Suspense or SWR for client-side data fetching with good loading states. The site detail page should feel fast — show site info immediately, then load the windgram. Consider prefetching profiles for favorited sites.

userStories.title: Multi-Day Dashboard Thumbnails
userStories.description: On the dashboard, show small windgram thumbnails for each favorited site giving an at-a-glance 7-day overview.
acceptanceCriteria: Dashboard shows each favorited/custom site as a card
Each card has a small windgram thumbnail (simplified: just lapse rate colors, no barbs) for today
Flyability rating badge on each card (Epic/Great/Good/Fair/Poor)
7-day mini bar showing flyability rating for each day (colored dots or small bars)
Click card → navigate to full site detail page with full windgram
Cards are responsive: 1 column on mobile, 2-3 on desktop
Performance: thumbnails should load quickly (simplified rendering, smaller data fetch)
npm run build succeeds
npm run lint passes
userStories.notes: Thumbnails are a simplified version of the windgram — just the background colors, maybe 100x60px. Don't fetch full atmospheric profiles for thumbnails; use the existing simple forecast data for the 7-day bar and only the first day's profile for the thumbnail. Lazy-load thumbnails as they scroll into view.
